localizedDescriptions.en: Document to communicate information to others, commonly in response to a request from another party.
The name or type of the health item under investigation.
The type of report.

annotations.comment: For example: hospital investigation; or community investigation.

localizedDescriptions.en: An individual human being.

children.name: Structured name of investigator

localizedDescriptions.en: Discrete components of an individual's name.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.structured_name.v1 and name/value='Structured name of investigator']

children.name: Title

localizedDescriptions.en: One or more honorific form(s) of address commencing a name.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.structured_name.v1 and name/value='Structured name of investigator']/items[at0001]/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element are set to 0..* to allow for more than one Title to be recorded. Coding with an external terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: 'Doctor'; 'Ms'; 'Mx'; or 'Professor Dr'.

children.name: First name

localizedDescriptions.en: One or more unique name(s) used to identify an individual within a family group.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.structured_name.v1 and name/value='Structured name of investigator']/items[at0002 and name/value='First name']/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element are set to 0..* to allow for more than one Given name to be recorded. In addition, this data element may be cloned and renamed within a template to allow discrete recording of different types of Given name - for example 'First name', 'Middle name', 'Preferred name' or 'Nickname', as required for a specific use case.

children.name: Last name

localizedDescriptions.en: One or more name(s) that an individual has in common with a family group.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.structured_name.v1 and name/value='Structured name of investigator']/items[at0005 and name/value='Last name']/value

annotations.comment: Also known as 'Last name' or 'Surname'. Occurrences for this data element are set to 0..* to allow for more than one Family name to be recorded. Complex names such as 'El Haddad' or 'van der Heyden' can be recorded using this naming pattern, as identified in ISO 22220 (Annex F), but for the intended use case for this archetype it is most likely that the full family name will be recorded as a string.

children.name: Professional license number

localizedDescriptions.en: Identifier associated with the individual.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[at0003 and name/value='Professional license number']

children.name: Professional license number

localizedDescriptions.en: Identifier associated with the individual.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[at0003 and name/value='Professional license number']/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element is set to 0..* to allow for more than one Identifier to be recorded. Note that the DV_IDENTIFIER data type contains multiple subcomponents for recording the ID value, type, issuer and assigned. For example - social security number; driver's license; or passport number.

children.name: Professional license number

localizedDescriptions.en: Identifier associated with the individual.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[at0003 and name/value='Professional license number']/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element is set to 0..* to allow for more than one Identifier to be recorded. Note that the DV_IDENTIFIER data type contains multiple subcomponents for recording the ID value, type, issuer and assigned. For example - social security number; driver's license; or passport number.

Occurrences for this data element is set to 0..* to allow for more than one Identifier to be recorded. Note that the DV_IDENTIFIER data type contains multiple subcomponents for recording the ID value, type, issuer and assigned. For example - social security number; driver's license; or passport number.

children.name: Professional group

localizedDescriptions.en: The relationship or role of the individual to the subject of the health record.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person.v1 and name/value='Case investigator']/items[at0004 and name/value='Professional group']/value

annotations.comment: For example - the copyholder of an advance care record; contact person in an organisation; relative in a family history entry; specimen collector; or witness to a fall or accident. If the individual being described using this archetype is the subject of the health record, then this data element is redundant.

localizedDescriptions.en: The relationship or role of the individual to the subject of the health record.

annotations.comment: For example - the copyholder of an advance care record; contact person in an organisation; relative in a family history entry; specimen collector; or witness to a fall or accident. If the individual being described using this archetype is the subject of the health record, then this data element is redundant.

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about a specified type of electronic communication.
The type or form of electronic communication.

annotations.comment: The DV_CODED_TEXT value set supports recording of the most common electronic communications used in health. If other alternatives are required the DV_TEXT data type can be used to record other types of electronic communication such as social media or video conferencing URLs within a template.

localizedDescriptions.en: The unique combination of alphanumeric characters, relevant for representation of 'Type'.
The unique combination of alphanumeric characters, relevant for representation of 'Type'.

annotations.comment: For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.

localizedDescriptions.en: The unique combination of alphanumeric characters, relevant for representation of 'Type'.

annotations.comment: For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.
For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about a specified type of electronic communication.
The type or form of electronic communication.
For SMS or voice calls.

annotations.comment: The DV_CODED_TEXT value set supports recording of the most common electronic communications used in health. If other alternatives are required the DV_TEXT data type can be used to record other types of electronic communication such as social media or video conferencing URLs within a template.

localizedDescriptions.en: The unique combination of alphanumeric characters, relevant for representation of 'Type'.
The unique combination of alphanumeric characters, relevant for representation of 'Type'.

annotations.comment: For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.

annotations.comment: For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.
For example: area code + landline phone/pager number; country code + mobile phone number; or email address.

localizedDescriptions.en: Description about one or more source types for the information contained in the report.

annotations.comment: For example: 'hospital record'; or 'interview with family'.

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the local health region where the index case was identified or the index event happened.

annotations.comment: This data element supports reporting by a jurisdictional grouping rather than a specific facility or institution, place or physical address. This data element may be able to be derived from the precise recording of 'Investigation site'.

localizedDescriptions.en: The date when the investigation report was completed.
Date when the final report was submitted to the final health authority.
A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
Summary information about the death of an individual, excluding details about the cause(s) of death.

children.name: Date & time of death

localizedDescriptions.en: Date and or time of death.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[at0092 and name/value='Date & time of death']

children.name: Date & time of death

localizedDescriptions.en: Date and or time of death.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[at0092 and name/value='Date & time of death']/value

annotations.comment: For example: based on findings pertaining to examination of the corpse and the pathologist's reconstruction of time of death based on post-mortem changes, temperature, etc.

children.name: Date & time of death

localizedDescriptions.en: Date and or time of death.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[at0092 and name/value='Date & time of death']/value

annotations.comment: For example: based on findings pertaining to examination of the corpse and the pathologist's reconstruction of time of death based on post-mortem changes, temperature, etc.
For example: based on findings pertaining to examination of the corpse and the pathologist's reconstruction of time of death based on post-mortem changes, temperature, etc.

localizedDescriptions.en: The category of the place where the individual died.

annotations.comment: This data element has multiple occurrences to allow more than one level of category, including subcategories about the place of death. It is recommended that 'Place category' be coded with an external terminology. For example: hospital or home; or, within the context of a hospital: ICU; ward; or operating theatre.

localizedDescriptions.en: The category of the place where the individual died.

annotations.comment: This data element has multiple occurrences to allow more than one level of category, including subcategories about the place of death. It is recommended that 'Place category' be coded with an external terminology. For example: hospital or home; or, within the context of a hospital: ICU; ward; or operating theatre.

localizedDescriptions.en: The simple name of, or description about, the place where the individual died.

annotations.comment: For example: 'City Hospital'; 'Mother's house'; or 'Eiffel tower'. This data element has multiple occurrences to allow more than one level of detail about the place of death, such as recording both 'City Hospital' and 'Ward 6 North'.

children.name: Address of place

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about the location of a person, physical building or landmark.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address.v1 and name/value='Address of place']

children.name: Address line

localizedDescriptions.en: An unstructured address line representing all relevant street-level or post-box details that would support the identification of a location.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address.v1 and name/value='Address of place']/items[at0001 and name/value='Address line']/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element is set to 0..* to allow one or more free text 'Address line' data elements to represent the huge variety of ways that ‘street-level' details may need to be recorded within the context of a specified 'Town'. Multiple address lines can be represented in a template, renamed as 'Address line 1', 'Address line 2,' etc. For example: '7A/52 Davis Street' or 'Apartment 7A' as Address line 1 with '52 Davis Street' as Address line 2; a roadside mail box location, such as 'RMB 725, Princes Highway'; or using a descriptive landmark, such as or 'Corner of Smith & Brown Streets' or 'Second house north of the general store with the red door'.

children.name: Landmark

localizedDescriptions.en: An unstructured address line representing all relevant street-level or post-box details that would support the identification of a location.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address.v1 and name/value='Address of place']/items[at0001 and name/value='Landmark']/value

annotations.comment: Occurrences for this data element is set to 0..* to allow one or more free text 'Address line' data elements to represent the huge variety of ways that ‘street-level' details may need to be recorded within the context of a specified 'Town'. Multiple address lines can be represented in a template, renamed as 'Address line 1', 'Address line 2,' etc. For example: '7A/52 Davis Street' or 'Apartment 7A' as Address line 1 with '52 Davis Street' as Address line 2; a roadside mail box location, such as 'RMB 725, Princes Highway'; or using a descriptive landmark, such as or 'Corner of Smith & Brown Streets' or 'Second house north of the general store with the red door'.

children.name: Community

localizedDescriptions.en: The name of the suburb, town, city, village, community or lowest level locality that contains the address.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address.v1 and name/value='Address of place']/items[at0002 and name/value='Community']/value

annotations.comment: Coding with an external terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: 'Fitzroy'; 'Manchester'; 'Kingston' or 'Bergen'.

children.name: Parish

localizedDescriptions.en: The name of a local government district or geographical area that contains the address.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Case context']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.death_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address.v1 and name/value='Address of place']/items[at0003 and name/value='Parish']/value

annotations.comment: Coding with an external terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: 'Surf Coast' and 'Yarra' local government councils in Victoria, Australia; and the Counties of 'Cornwall', 'Middlesex' & 'Surrey' in Jamaica.

localizedDescriptions.en: The age of the individual at the time of death.
Timing of the death of a woman related to a pregnancy and up to one year postpartum.
The woman died while pregnant and before labour.
The woman died during labour and/or delivery.
The woman died ≤42 days after the end of a pregnancy.
The woman died >42 days and ≤1 year after the end of a pregnancy.
The number of days from the end of a pregnancy to death.
A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
Summary details about the antenatal care received during a single pregnancy.

children.name: High Risk Clinic summary
children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Pregnancy care']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.pregnancy_care_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[at0080 and name/value='High Risk Clinic summary']

children.localizedName: Number of High Risk Clinic visits

localizedDescriptions.en: The number of visits to the identified clinic.

localizedDescriptions.en: Significant issues, problems or diagnoses managed by the specified type of care.

annotations.comment: Clone to enable more specific recording of 'Routine' from 'Specialised' ambulatory antenatal care.

localizedDescriptions.en: Total number of antenatal clinic visits of all types and locations.
Inpatient episode_details

The scheduled date for antenatal admission followup.
Inpatient episode_details

children.name: Date of discharge
children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Pregnancy care']/items[openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.pregnancy_care_summary.v0]/data[at0001]/items[at0058]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.inpatient_episode_details.v0]/items[at0005 and name/value='Date of discharge']/value

localizedDescriptions.en: Inpatient episode_details
Significant issues, problems or diagnoses managed by the specified type of care.
The scheduled date for postnatal admission followup.
Evidence of follow up about the escalation of care.
An entity comprising one or more people and having a particular purpose.
The unstructured name or label for the organisation.

annotations.comment: For example: 'Royal Children's Hospital'; 'ABC District Nursing Service'; 'YNWA Oslo' or 'JB Smith Lawyers'.
For example: a company, institution, association, network, department, community group, healthcare practice group, payer/insurer, care team, or a group of neighbours who provide care or support.

localizedDescriptions.en: The date when the patient was referred for additional care.
The reason for escalation.
Overview or summary record of a pregnancy and outcome, including the antenatal period, labour, birth and the immediate postnatal period.
Outcome of the pregnancy as a whole.

annotations.comment: This data element is only used to record the outcome of a pregnancy, before it is useful to record an outcome on a per foetus basis. For example: a 'miscarriage' or a 'missed abortion'. Coding of the 'Early pregnancy outcome' with an external terminology is recommended. If it is necessary to record outcome information per infant or foetus use the 'Infant outcome' data element instead.  This data element is redundant if 'Infant outcome' has been recorded.

localizedDescriptions.en: The gestation when the pregnancy has ended.

annotations.comment: Also known as 'Gestation at birth' for pregnancies resulting in viable outcomes.

localizedDescriptions.en: Number of foetuses identified in utero.
Manner in which labour started.
No onset of labour.
Onset without intervention.
Onset through intervention.
Information about a single foetus or newborn infant.
Outcome of the pregnancy for the identified infant or foetus.

annotations.comment: This data element is only used to record the outcome of a pregnancy for a single infant or foetus. For example: 'Live birth'; or 'Stillbirth'. Coding of the 'Infant outcome' with an external terminology is recommended.  If it is necessary to record outcome information for the pregnancy as a whole use the 'Early pregnancy outcome' data element instead. This data element is redundant if 'Early pregnancy outcome' has been recorded.

localizedDescriptions.en: Sex of the infant  by direct observation of external genitalia.
Baby appears physically male.
Baby appears physically female.
Sex of the baby has not yet been able to be determined from observation of physical characteristics.

annotations.comment: Coding with a terminology is preferred, where possible. For example: Male; female; intersex; indeterminate. Indeterminate is to be used in the situation where the infant has ambiguous external genitalia. This data element may be used to populate the newborn health record using the 'Sex assigned at birth' data element within the EVALUATION.gender archetype.

localizedDescriptions.en: A subset of persistent or summary information about the pregnancy and birth of an infant, selected for utility of use within both the maternal and infant health records.
Method by which the newborn was delivered.

annotations.comment: For example: vaginal; forceps rotation; or Caesarean Section.

localizedDescriptions.en: The level of urgency identified at or around the time of delivery.
The delivery, induction or Caesarean Section did not require any unexpected or urgent intervention.
Recognition of an increasing risk situation required the delivery to be expedited.
Recognition of a high risk situation required the delivery to be expedited as an emergency.
Presenting part of the infant at delivery.

annotations.comment: The clinical context for recording this data element is only presentation at delivery, not at any other phase of labour.

localizedDescriptions.en: Weight of the infant at delivery.

annotations.comment: This data element may be used to populate the newborn health record using both the 'Weight' data element and 'Birth' EVENT within the OBSERVATION.body_weight archetype.

localizedDescriptions.en: Description of the outcome at the end of the neonatal period per newborn infant.
The infant survived the neonatal period.
The infant did not survive the neonatal period.
The age of the infant if they died during the neonatal  period.

annotations.comment: These data elements may be used to record details about live births or stillborn infants.

localizedDescriptions.en: Narrative summary or overview about a patient, specifically from the perspective of a healthcare provider, and with or without associated interpretations.
The summary, assessment, conclusions or evaluation of the clinical findings.
